title: Wind farms dry surface soil in temporal and spatial variation
author: Wang, Li, et al.

steps.title: Calculation method of the land surface temperature and NDVI
steps.instruction: To investigate the interannual variation in the soil moisture before and after the wind farms were built, as well as to minimize the influence of the model error and climate change on the research results, the method of Zhou et al. [8] was utilized. More specifically, the difference between the mean soil moisture in each season before (1984–2008) was determined and after the wind farms were built (2009–2022).

steps.title: Methods for evaluating spatiotemporal differences in the measured soil moisture
steps.instruction: First, the operation of the wind farm was judged based on the wind speed data, to exclude the influence of the difference in surface conditions on the results. Second, the wind position of each automatic weather station was judged based on the wind direction data. As a result, the upwind direction, downwind direction and crosswind direction were obtained. Since the crosswind direction was not on the parallel axis of the wind direction, it was the least affected. Hence, the crosswind direction was taken as the control.

steps.title: Limitations and uncertainties of the method
steps.instruction: The research method of this paper provides a reference for analysing the influence of wind farms on soil moisture. The conclusions provide a reference for further judging the influence of wind farms on local ecosystems. Although the influence extent of a wind farm on soil moisture may be different in different ecosystems and soil types, an overall reduction in soil moisture by wind farms does indeed occur.
